answer: Argon2id is used with 64MB memory, 3 iterations, and 4 degree of parallelism.
The refresh token expiration period is 7 days with a rotate-on-use policy.
The database is currently running version 15.4, having been upgraded from version 14.9.
5000 burst and 2000 steady.
Argon2id was chosen over bcrypt for its side-channel resistance.
Drizzle is used because it has zero runtime dependencies and provides a SQL-like TypeScript experience.
Both services (and all services in the system) verify auth tokens via JWKS located at /.well-known/jwks.json.
Rate limiting is done at the ALB to stop DDoS attacks before they reach the application containers.

The chain involves the Gateway (schema validation), the Lambda-authorizer (JWT validation via JWKS and claims injection), VPCLink, the ALB, and the Portfolio Service's RBAC middleware (role hierarchy).

If the JWKS endpoint goes down, all downstream authentication fails, though token issuance continues because the private key is local. There is a grace period provided by an LRU cache with 1000 entries and a 5-minute TTL.
Real-time portfolio alerting depends on both the Notification Service and a Gateway WebSocket API; it is currently planned for Q3.
Yes, it was resolved by using Unix timestamps instead of Date objects for token expiry.
Webhooks are implemented via POST /api/v1/notifications/webhooks/register, with an exponential backoff policy of 5 attempts over 24 hours.
100 (5 instances multiplied by a pool of 20 connections per instance).
NOT IN CONTEXT.
No, Fargate Spot is not used for the Auth Service because it is an always-on service; Spot is reserved for batch jobs (portfolio-recalc, digest) due to the risk of interruption.
NOT IN CONTEXT.
The 30-day backup retention supports the Disaster Recovery strategy, which includes an RPO of 1 hour and an RTO of 4 hours with replication to us-west-2.
The upgrade required changing the sslmode to verify-full and using global-bundle.pem.
The columns are id, account_id, security_id, acquisition_date, cost_basis, quantity, adjusted, and created_at.
If MFA is enabled, the flow requires TOTP verification or the use of a recovery code after handlePostLogin and Argon2id verification.
10 recovery codes are generated; they are single-use and permanently consumed upon use.
The policy is 5 attempts over 24 hours with exponential backoff.
At rest: PII uses AES-256-GCM with per-tenant keys, and passwords use Argon2id (64MB/3iter/4par). In transit: mTLS via Envoy and RDS connections use sslmode=verify-full with global-bundle.pem.
Gateway (schema validation) -> Lambda-authorizer (JWKS/claims) -> Auth Service (handlePostLogin, Argon2id verify, MFA/TOTP check) -> Issuance of RS256 access (15min) and refresh (7day) tokens.

Constraints include a 20-connection pool per instance, 1MB payload limit, mTLS via Envoy, and auth via JWKS. Patterns include handle{Verb}{Resource} naming, RFC7807 error details, Pino logging, AsyncLocalStorage request IDs, real PG testing with testcontainers, and Claude Code review.
The request passes the Gateway (within 5000 burst/2000 steady limits) but is throttled at the ALB layer, which has a limit of 1000/min/IP.
The Auth Service (private key local for issuance), the JWKS endpoint, the Gateway Lambda-authorizer, and every downstream service (Portfolio, Notification, etc.) that validates tokens.
The pipeline consists of GitHub Actions (lint, typecheck, test, build, ECR, ECS) with a Claude Code review before merge, followed by a zero-downtime blue-green ALB target switch.
